If an event U takes place at some Y and some X performs U then normally X is at Y. Why: assumed basic knowledge.
(1) If an event U takes place at some Y and some X performs U then normally X is at Y  [background knowledge, confidence 90%]
(2) The event act6 takes place at the hospital B  [sentence 1]
(3) If some X performs the event act6 then normally X is at the hospital B  [from steps 1, 2, confidence 90%]
(5) Normally John is at the hospital B  [from steps 3, 4, confidence 90%]
(6) If John is at some X then 'at X' is the answer  [sentence 2]
(8) If John is at some X then at is an answer  [from steps 6, 7]
(9) 'At the hospital B' is an answer, except when John is not at the hospital B  [from steps 5, 8, confidence 90%]
John is not at hospital B
